sso.permissionset.CfnAbout: Specifies a permission set within a specified IAM Identity Center instance.

sso.permissionset.CustomerManagedPolicyR.Description: Specifies the names and paths of the customer managed policies that you have attached to your permission set.

sso.permissionset.InlinePolicy.Description: The inline policy that is attached to the permission set.  For `Length Constraints`, if a valid ARN is provided for a permission set, it is possible for an empty inline policy to be returned.

sso.permissionset.InstanceArn.Description: The ARN of the IAM Identity Center instance under which the operation will be executed. For more information about ARNs, see [Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) and AWS Service Namespaces](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) in the *AWS General Reference*.

sso.permissionset.ManagedPolicies.Description: A structure that stores the details of the AWS managed policy.

sso.permissionset.Name.Description: The name of the permission set.

sso.permissionset.PermissionsBoundary.Description: Specifies the configuration of the AWS managed or customer managed policy that you want to set as a permissions boundary. Specify either `CustomerManagedPolicyReference` to use the name and path of a customer managed policy, or `ManagedPolicyArn` to use the ARN of an AWS managed policy. A permissions boundary represents the maximum permissions that any policy can grant your role. For more information, see [Permissions boundaries for IAM entities](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies_boundaries.html) in the *IAM User Guide*.  Policies used as permissions boundaries don't provide permissions. You must also attach an IAM policy to the role. To learn how the effective permissions for a role are evaluated, see [IAM JSON policy evaluation logic](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/reference_policies_evaluation-logic.html) in the *IAM User Guide*.

sso.permissionset.RelayStateType.Description: Used to redirect users within the application during the federation authentication process.

sso.permissionset.SessionDuration.Description: The length of time that the application user sessions are valid for in the ISO-8601 standard.

sso.permissionset.Tags.Description: The tags to attach to the new [AWS::SSO::PermissionSet](#aws-resource-sso-permissionset).
